region_of_origin: Philippines (adapted for Singapore fast food market)
food_subgroup: Pasta dish with meat sauce, served as a fast-food platter

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Filipino-style sweet spaghetti with meat sauce platter. FOODON_00002473 (pasta food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Filipino-style spaghetti with sweet meat sauce as a composite fast-food platter.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229961001 refers to spaghetti as a substance; used as closest parent since no code exists for the full platter preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Jollibee Spaghetti Platter serving is approximately 400 g, comprising a generous portion of cooked spaghetti topped with sweet Filipino-style meat sauce and grated cheese.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with meat sauce, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 27116) adapted with Filipino-style sweet sauce composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for spaghetti with meat sauce (NDB 27116). Values adjusted upward for sugar content to reflect Filipino-style sweet tomato sauce characteristic of Jollibee spaghetti, which contains added sugar and hotdog slices. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values are consistent with USDA SR Legacy composite pasta-with-meat-sauce data. Sodium adjusted to reflect fast-food preparation levels. This is an adapted estimate; exact Jollibee Singapore nutritional data is not available in any approved primary source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — spaghetti, boiled, with meat sauce (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee-style Filipino sweet spaghetti platter. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for spaghetti with meat sauce (boiled, white pasta), which reports GI values in the range 40–50 (low category). The sweet tomato sauce adds sugar but the dominant glycemic driver remains the pasta matrix. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 74.0) / 100 = 33.3, where carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 74.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1520 mg per platter serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content from processed meat (hotdog slices), tomato sauce, and fast-food preparation, which can significantly contribute to daily sodium intake exceeding the 2000 mg recommended limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (45) but high glycemic load per serving (~33) due to large portion size and added sugar in sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this dish; while the GI is low-to-moderate for pasta, the large serving size and added sugar in the Filipino-style sweet sauce produce a high glycemic load per platter, which may cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ground pork and processed hotdog slices, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its saturated fat content from processed meat ingredients and its high sodium level, both of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Spaghetti Platter is a Filipino fast-food adaptation of Italian spaghetti, served as a full meal platter at Jollibee restaurants including the Bugis Village outlet in Singapore. Unlike traditional Italian spaghetti bolognese, the Filipino version is characterised by a distinctly sweet tomato-based meat sauce made with ground pork, sliced hotdog pieces, and a generous amount of sugar, giving it a sweeter flavour profile that is popular in the Philippines and among Filipino diaspora communities. The dish is topped with grated cheese, adding a mild savoury and creamy contrast to the sweet sauce. The pasta is boiled spaghetti noodles, soft in texture, coated thoroughly with the rich, glossy sauce. Nutritionally, the platter provides moderate protein from the meat components, significant carbohydrate from the pasta, and notable sodium from the processed meat and sauce. The glycemic index of the pasta base is low-to-moderate (approximately 45), but the large serving size results in a high glycemic load per platter. The dish is classified as halal at Jollibee Singapore. It is high in sodium (~1520 mg per platter), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The sweet sauce contributes added sugar, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. This dish is a culturally significant comfort food for the Filipino community in Singapore and is widely recognised as a defining menu item of the Jollibee brand.
common_user_queries: Is Jollibee spaghetti halal in Singapore?
How many calories in Jollibee spaghetti platter?
What is the difference between Filipino spaghetti and Italian spaghetti?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for Jollibee Singapore Spaghetti Platter (D) is available in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for spaghetti with meat sauce, with adjustments for the Filipino-style sweet sauce composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for spaghetti with meat sauce. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (spaghetti with meat sauce, NDB 27116) with upward sugar adjustment for Filipino-style sweet tomato sauce. Exact Jollibee Singapore nutritional panel data was not available in any approved primary source at time of processing. 2. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for a full platter; actual weight may vary. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 6. The dish is tagged as Halal based on Jollibee Singapore's halal certification status; this should be verified against current certification records. 7. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for spaghetti with meat sauce; no direct measurement exists for this specific product. 8. Glycemic load per serving = (45 × 74.0) / 100 = 33.3, based on estimated 74 g carbohydrate in a 400 g serving. 9. The food_id GD000504 is preserved from the input seed record. 10. This record is classified under Fast Food cuisine as per the input source (field survey data, Jollibee - Bugis Village). Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore.
